subject: computer-science
description: Famous Computer Scientists MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Who is known as the Father of Computers?
mcqs.explanation: Charles Babbage is known as the Father of Computers. He designed the Analytical Engine in the 1830s — the first general-purpose mechanical computer.

mcqs.q: Charles Babbage is famous for designing which machine?
options: The Difference Engine and Analytical Engine
mcqs.explanation: Charles Babbage is famous for designing the Difference Engine and the Analytical Engine — the latter is considered the first general-purpose mechanical computer.

mcqs.q: Who is widely considered the world's first computer programmer?
mcqs.explanation: Ada Lovelace is widely considered the world's first computer programmer — she wrote an algorithm for Babbage's Analytical Engine.

mcqs.q: Who proposed the concept of the Turing Machine and is considered a father of theoretical computer science?
mcqs.explanation: Alan Turing proposed the Turing Machine and is considered a father of theoretical computer science. He also helped break the Enigma code in WWII.

mcqs.q: Who is famous for the von Neumann architecture (stored-program computer)?
mcqs.explanation: John von Neumann is famous for the von Neumann architecture — the stored-program computer design used by virtually all modern computers.

mcqs.q: Who is known as the Father of Information Theory?
mcqs.explanation: Claude Shannon is known as the Father of Information Theory — his 1948 paper laid the foundation of digital communication.

mcqs.q: Who coined the term 'Artificial Intelligence' and created LISP?
mcqs.explanation: John McCarthy coined the term 'Artificial Intelligence' in 1956 at the Dartmouth Conference and created LISP — a key AI programming language.

mcqs.q: Which scientist is famous for the DENDRAL project and expert systems in AI?
mcqs.explanation: Edward A. Feigenbaum is famous for the DENDRAL project and pioneering work on expert systems in AI.

mcqs.q: Who is famous for Bayesian Networks and causal reasoning in AI?
mcqs.explanation: Judea Pearl is famous for probability theory, causal reasoning and Bayesian Networks — foundations of modern AI.

mcqs.q: Which scientist is famous for speech recognition research in AI?
mcqs.explanation: Dabbala B. ('Raj') Reddy is famous for artificial intelligence and speech recognition research.

mcqs.q: Who invented the World Wide Web (WWW) and the first web browser?
mcqs.explanation: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989 along with the first web browser, HTML and HTTP.

mcqs.q: Tim Berners-Lee is associated with which of the following?
options: World Wide Web, HTTP, network design
mcqs.explanation: Tim Berners-Lee is associated with network design, the World Wide Web (WWW) and HTTP.

mcqs.q: Who jointly developed the TCP/IP protocol — the foundation of the modern Internet?
mcqs.explanation: Vinton G. Cerf and Robert E. Kahn jointly developed the TCP/IP protocol — they are sometimes called the 'fathers of the Internet'.

mcqs.q: Vinton G. Cerf is most famous for:
mcqs.explanation: Vinton G. Cerf is most famous for network design and co-inventing TCP/IP.

mcqs.q: Robert E. Kahn is most famous for:
mcqs.explanation: Robert E. Kahn is most famous for co-developing the TCP/IP protocol along with Vinton Cerf.

mcqs.q: Radia J. Perlman is famous for:
options: Network design including the Spanning Tree Protocol (STP)
mcqs.explanation: Radia J. Perlman is famous for network design — particularly the Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) and TORTIS.

mcqs.q: Butler W. Lampson contributed to which of the following?
mcqs.explanation: Butler W. Lampson contributed to network design, time-sharing systems, the SDS 940 and the Xerox Alto.

mcqs.q: Who created the C programming language and was a co-creator of UNIX?
mcqs.explanation: Dennis Ritchie created the C programming language and was a co-creator of UNIX (along with Ken Thompson) at AT&T Bell Labs.

mcqs.q: Kenneth L. Thompson is famous for:
options: The B programming language and UNIX (with Dennis Ritchie)
mcqs.explanation: Kenneth L. Thompson is famous for the B programming language and for co-creating UNIX along with Dennis Ritchie.

mcqs.q: Who designed the Pascal programming language?
mcqs.explanation: Niklaus E. Wirth designed Pascal — to teach structured programming without GOTO statements.

mcqs.q: Who developed the C++ programming language?
mcqs.explanation: Bjarne Stroustrup developed C++ at AT&T in the mid-1980s — extending C with object-oriented features.

mcqs.q: Who designed the Java programming language?
mcqs.explanation: James A. Gosling is famous for designing the Java programming language at Sun Microsystems in the early 1990s.

mcqs.q: Who developed the Python programming language?
mcqs.explanation: Guido van Rossum (Netherlands) developed Python and released it in 1991.

mcqs.q: Anders Hejlsberg developed which programming language at Microsoft?
mcqs.explanation: Anders Hejlsberg developed C# (C Sharp) at Microsoft in 2000.

mcqs.q: Who created the Linux operating system?
mcqs.explanation: Linus B. Torvalds created the Linux operating system in 1991 — now the most widely used open-source OS.

mcqs.q: C. Antony (Tony) Hoare is famous for:
options: ALGOL-W, Quicksort, CSP and Hoare Logic
mcqs.explanation: C. Antony Hoare is famous for ALGOL-W, the Quicksort algorithm, CSP (Communicating Sequential Processes) and Hoare Logic.

mcqs.q: Alan Kay is famous for which of the following?
options: Object-oriented languages, mobile computing and the windowing GUI
mcqs.explanation: Alan Kay is famous for object-oriented programming languages, mobile computing and the windowing GUI (Smalltalk).

mcqs.q: Barbara Liskov is famous for which programming-languages concept?
options: CLU language and the Liskov Substitution Principle
mcqs.explanation: Barbara Liskov is famous for the CLU and Argus languages and the Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP) in OOP.

mcqs.q: Frederick P. Brooks Jr. is famous for which operating system?
mcqs.explanation: Frederick P. Brooks Jr. is famous for managing the development of IBM's OS/360 and the book 'The Mythical Man-Month'.

mcqs.q: Fernando J. Corbató is famous for:
mcqs.explanation: Fernando J. Corbató is famous for operating systems and time-sharing systems.

mcqs.q: Who is famous for the Logo programming language for education?
mcqs.explanation: Cynthia Solomon is famous for AI, programming languages and (along with Seymour Papert) the Logo programming language for education.

mcqs.q: Who wrote 'The Art of Computer Programming'?
mcqs.explanation: Donald E. Knuth wrote 'The Art of Computer Programming' — the seminal multi-volume work on algorithms.

mcqs.q: Who is famous for the Hopcroft-Karp algorithm and formal languages/automata?
mcqs.explanation: John E. Hopcroft is famous for algorithms, formal languages and automata — including the Hopcroft-Karp algorithm and contributions to neural networks.

mcqs.q: Richard M. Karp is famous for:
options: Computational complexity, Edmonds-Karp and Hopcroft-Karp algorithms
mcqs.explanation: Richard M. Karp is famous for computational complexity theory, the Edmonds-Karp and Hopcroft-Karp algorithms, and bioinformatics.

mcqs.q: Stephen A. Cook is famous for:
options: Computational complexity theory and proof complexity
mcqs.explanation: Stephen A. Cook is famous for math/logic, computational complexity theory and proof complexity (the Cook-Levin theorem on NP-completeness).

mcqs.q: Robert E. Tarjan is famous for:
options: Splay Trees, Fibonacci Heaps and Tarjan's algorithms
mcqs.explanation: Robert E. Tarjan is famous for Splay Trees, Fibonacci Heaps and Tarjan's algorithms (off-line lowest common ancestors etc.).

mcqs.q: Who is famous for distributed systems theory, logical clocks and LaTeX?
mcqs.explanation: Leslie Lamport is famous for the theory of distributed systems, logical clocks, the Chandy-Lamport algorithm and LaTeX.

mcqs.q: Manuel Blum is famous for which web-security innovation?
mcqs.explanation: Manuel Blum is famous for computational complexity theory, Blum-Goldwasser cryptography and CAPTCHA.

mcqs.q: David A. Patterson is famous for:
mcqs.explanation: David A. Patterson is famous for the RISC microprocessor architecture and the RAID storage system.

mcqs.q: John L. Hennessy contributed to which of the following?
mcqs.explanation: John L. Hennessy contributed to RISC microprocessors and the FLASH multiprocessor.

mcqs.q: Ivan E. Sutherland is famous for:
options: Computer graphics, interactive interfaces and real-time computing
mcqs.explanation: Ivan E. Sutherland is famous for computer graphics (Sketchpad), interactive interfaces and real-time computing.

mcqs.q: Michael R. Stonebraker is famous for which of the following?
mcqs.explanation: Michael R. Stonebraker is famous for database management systems — Ingres, C-Store, H-Store and SciDB.

mcqs.q: What does RSA in cryptography stand for?
options: The surnames of Rivest, Shamir and Adleman
mcqs.explanation: RSA stands for the surnames of its three inventors: Ron Rivest, Adi Shamir and Leonard Adleman.

mcqs.q: Who developed the Diffie-Hellman key exchange?
mcqs.explanation: Whitfield Diffie and Martin E. Hellman developed the Diffie-Hellman key exchange — a foundation of public-key cryptography.

mcqs.q: Who is famous for the Miller-Rabin primality test?
mcqs.explanation: Michael O. Rabin is famous for the Miller-Rabin primality test, nondeterministic finite automata and cryptography.

mcqs.q: Adi Shamir is famous for which of the following?
options: RSA, Differential Cryptanalysis and Shamir Secret-Sharing
mcqs.explanation: Adi Shamir is famous for RSA, Differential Cryptanalysis and Shamir Secret-Sharing Scheme.

mcqs.q: Whitfield Diffie is famous for:
mcqs.explanation: Whitfield Diffie is famous for public-key cryptography and asymmetric-key algorithms (Diffie-Hellman).

mcqs.q: Shafi Goldwasser is famous for:
options: Computational complexity, Blum-Goldwasser cryptography, computational number theory
mcqs.explanation: Shafira (Shafi) Goldwasser is famous for computational complexity theory, Blum-Goldwasser cryptography and computational number theory.

mcqs.q: Silvio Micali is famous for which cryptographic concept?
options: Public-key cryptosystems, pseudorandom functions and zero-knowledge proofs
mcqs.explanation: Silvio Micali is famous for public-key cryptosystems, pseudorandom functions and zero-knowledge proofs.

mcqs.q: Who proposed the Quantum Mechanical Hamiltonian Model of a Turing Machine?
mcqs.explanation: Paul A. Benioff proposed the Quantum Mechanical Hamiltonian Model of a Turing Machine — laying foundations for quantum computing.

mcqs.q: Who is famous for the Quantum Turing Machine and quantum algorithms?
mcqs.explanation: David Deutsch is famous for the Quantum Turing Machine, quantum information theory and quantum algorithms.

mcqs.q: What are the DiVincenzo Criteria associated with?
options: Theoretical requirements on physical implementations of quantum computers
mcqs.explanation: The DiVincenzo Criteria, by David P. DiVincenzo, list the theoretical requirements on physical implementations of quantum computers.

mcqs.q: Holevo's Theorem belongs to:
mcqs.explanation: Alexander Holevo is famous for Holevo's Theorem in quantum communication channel theory and quantum statistics.

mcqs.q: William K. Wootters is associated with:
options: The No-Cloning Theorem and quantum teleportation
mcqs.explanation: William K. Wootters is associated with quantum information theory — the No-Cloning Theorem, quantum teleportation and quantum cryptography.

mcqs.q: Charles H. Bennett is famous for:
options: Quantum information theory, quantum cryptography, quantum teleportation
mcqs.explanation: Charles H. Bennett is famous for logically reversible computation, algorithmic information theory, quantum information theory, quantum cryptography and quantum teleportation.
